mysteries.title: Dead on Arrival
mysteries.intro: A man is found dead in the middle of a frozen lake. A trail of blood leads from the treeline. In his hand — a phone, 911 dialed but never sent.
mysteries.flavor: Three friends were with him for the weekend. They say they were out on a beer run when it happened. Someone is lying. Or maybe no one is.
mysteries.spoiler: Jeffrey was alone in the cabin when he tripped on the uneven hearthstone and fell onto the fire poker. His friends were 20 minutes away — the receipt proves it. He walked onto the frozen lake looking for a signal strong enough to call for help. He never made it.

suspects.name: Tyler
suspects.role: Groom-to-be. His cabin, his bachelor party, his best friend just died.

topics.label: Ask about Jeffrey
topics.response: He was going to be my best man. I can't believe this happened. He can barely get the words out.

topics.label: Ask about the cabin
topics.response: It belongs to my in-laws. They already hate me for choosing Jeffrey as best man instead of Paul. I don't know what to do now.

topics.label: Ask about the wedding
topics.response: What about it — I'm getting married in two months and now my best man is dead. I don't want to cancel but this feels like a sign.

suspects.name: Paul
suspects.role: Tyler's future brother-in-law. Passed over for best man. Stayed at the cabin while the others left.

topics.label: Ask about the best man situation
topics.response: Hmph. I don't even care. My sister is marrying a loser and his friends all think he's so cool. Being best man is whatever. He won't make eye contact.

topics.label: Ask about the cabin
topics.response: I can't believe this happened. My parents are going to kill me. Or maybe say the wedding is off — which would not be a bad thing. I've been trying to reach them but there's no signal out here.

topics.label: Ask why he didn't go on the beer run
topics.response: It's my parents' cabin. I needed to stay and make sure things were fine. I was on the porch most of the time — freezing. I needed to clean some stuff. I don't know, man. He's agitated.

suspects.name: Omar
suspects.role: The wild card. Suggested the beer run. Got everyone out of the cabin.

topics.label: Ask about the beer run
topics.response: It was party time and Paul forgot to get drinks. I wanted poker, but Jeffrey was going sober — so we figured we'd have a little fun without making it weird for him.

topics.label: Ask about the weekend
topics.response: My boy is getting married — clearly for the money, but still. Jeffrey wanted this whole trip so bad. Honestly? I think he was going to ask Tyler for money after the wedding.

topics.label: Ask about Jeffrey
topics.response: Jeffrey's a guy with no job, no prospects. Owns a phone, that's about it. Tyler still treats him like they're in college. A pause. I just mean... he had a lot riding on Tyler.

clues.label: The fire poker
clues.response: There's dried blood along the lower half of the shaft. It wasn't thrown — it's right where it would normally rest by the fireplace.

clues.label: Fireplace stonework
clues.response: One of the hearthstones sits slightly higher than the rest. Maybe an inch. Easy to miss in dim light. Easy to catch your foot on.

clues.label: A gold ring on the table
clues.response: A gold band. Engraved on the inside: T + M. A wedding ring — but Jeffrey wasn't married.

clues.label: A crumpled receipt
clues.response: A gas station receipt. Two six-packs and a bag of chips. Timestamp: 9:47 PM. The store is 20 minutes from here.

accusations.label: Tyler — his cabin, his fire poker
